title: PHASE-005: Load Testing with k6
summary: Real k6 load test of Sprint-19 critical paths (lead ingestion, tRPC reads, and the DDD-001/002 atomic lead->deal conversion) against a local, prod-safe dev-auth target. Canonical 3-min run met the ADR-018 budget with margin: 1,081 leads created (~21,617/hour, 4.3x the 5,000/hour budget), p95 11.6ms (<200ms), 0% errors (<0.1%), 180 atomic conversions (0 failures). Identified bottleneck: per-user AUTHENTICATED rate-limit tier (1,000 req/60s, keyed by userId) — a single-user synthetic-harness artifact, not a system ceiling. Tenant isolation (RLS) verified at the DB layer under a non-superuser role. Evidence: real k6 HTML report (real timestamps), Grafana/Prometheus screenshot, summary JSON.

gate_results.detail: load-test-report.html is a k6 web-dashboard export from an actual run (real timestamps); grafana-load-test.png captured from Grafana over Prometheus remote-write. No template report (RISK-S19-02).
p95 11.6ms (ingestion); per-path max ~21ms. k6 threshold http_req_duration{scenario:lead_ingestion} p(95)<200 held.
0 errors / 2,343 requests (0%). k6 threshold http_req_failed rate<0.001 held; k6 exit 0, no threshold crossings.
Per-user AUTHENTICATED rate limit (1,000/60s, userId-keyed): over-drive at ~26 req/s single-user drew uniform 36% 429 (harness artifact). Documented in bottleneck-analysis.md.
Local apps/api :4000 + dev-auth fallback + local test DB. Never hit prod Supabase.
`pnpm test:load` script added (run-ifc-033-load-test.mjs) and used for the canonical run; wired as a secret-free job in .github/workflows/performance-gate.yml.

notes: 5,000 leads/hour was validated against the ADR-018 MVP budget; the aspirational 1,000-VU/p99<100ms target remains IFC-047 (Sprint 22). Target ran under tsx (dev) — a compiled build would only be faster. IFC-034 (Gate-3 £3000 review) is the HUMAN decision gate downstream; this task supplies its evidence and does not itself require Gate-3 sign-off to merge.
